steps.description: Pre-grinding for 15 min, grinding Ni(NO3)2-6H2O and homogenization of the mixture
Grinding for 30 min (2 x 15 min), heating of the system, resulting starch gelation and better homogenization of the sample
Pyrolysis process

human_recipe.notes: The molar ratio of SiO2/starch/Ni2+ was 1:0.3:0. Additionally, to examine the effect of mechanochemical treatment on porous structure of the matrix, the initial silicas were subjected to mechanochemical treatment under the same conditions and they are denoted A-200 M and S-50 M. The pyrolysis was carried out in a rotary quartz reactor in nitrogen atmosphere (120 cm3 min-1 ) using the following procedure: heating from 20 to 500 C (10 C min-1 ), isothermal annealing at 500 C (3 h) and cooling to room temperature. The samples obtained in this way are denoted with the general formulae SSNi-x (for the samples prepared on the base of Sipernat) and ASNi-x (for the samples prepared on the base of Aerosil, where x = 0, 0.1, …, 0.5).

steps.description: Preparation of the mixtures takes place using mechanochemistry as a method of carbonaceous matter and catalyst application. The molar ratio of SiO2/starch/Ni2+ was 1:0.3:0.1
Mechanochemical treatment of prepared mixtures

equipment.name: Planetary Mill, PULVERISETTE 7
equipment.settings: pre-grinding for 15 min, 500 rpm [grinding Ni(NO3)2-6H2O and homogenization of the mixture], followed by grinding for 30 min (2 × 15 min), 750 rpm (heating of the system, resulting starch gelation and better homogenization of the sample)

steps.description: All the silica/starch/Ni(NO3)2 mixtures obtained mechanochemically were subjected to the pyrolysis process.
heating from 20 to 500 C
isothermal annealing at 500 C
cooling to room temperature
